title: cochlearia officinalis subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Cochlearia officinalis subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean scurvy grass, is a subspecies of the sea-purse or scurvy grass plant that is native to the Pyrenees mountain range between France and Spain. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Pyrenean scurvy grass include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues.
